req.title: Patient portal login (audit)
req.statement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be rate-limited and notify the patient. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct test-worthy behaviours: (1) a successful SSO login that establishes a session and emits an AuditEvent (positive / EP), (2) repeated failed attempts that trigger HTTP 429 rate-limiting and patient notification (negative / NEG), and (3) the exact boundary at which rate-limiting kicks in — the Nth attempt that is still allowed vs the (N+1)th that is blocked (boundary / BVA). A fourth test covers the full state transition from unauthenticated → authenticating → authenticated as mandated by the AuditEvent end-to-end traceability clause (STC). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that reset server-side state before execution.

tests.name: test_successful_sso_login_establishes_session_and_emits_audit_event
preconditions: Valid SSO assertion is available for the test patient
AuditEvent FHIR endpoint is accessible with admin credentials
Rate-limit counter for test patient is reset
actions: POST a valid SSO assertion to /portal/login
Capture the HTTP response status and session token
Query the FHIR AuditEvent endpoint for the patient's login event
expected: HTTP 200 response with a non-empty session token
Response body contains authenticated patient identifier
AuditEvent of type 'rest' with outcome '0' (success) exists for this login
trace: R-HLT-098: session established on valid SSO
R-HLT-098: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_successful_sso_login_establishes_session_and_emits_audit_event(
    reset_rate_limit, valid_sso_assertion, api_client, audit_query
):
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/portal/login",
        json={"sso_assertion": valid_sso_assertion}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("session_token"), "session_token must be non-empty"
    assert body.get("patient_id"), "patient_id must be present"

    event = audit_query(
        patient_id=body["patient_id"],
        action="login",
        outcome="0"
    )
    assert event is not None, "AuditEvent with outcome success must exist"
    assert event["type"]["code"] == "rest"

tests.name: test_excessive_failed_logins_returns_429_and_notifies_patient
preconditions: Rate-limit counter for the test patient is reset to zero
Notification service stub is active and queryable
MAX_ATTEMPTS threshold is known (imported from config as MAX_ATTEMPTS)
actions: Send MAX_ATTEMPTS+1 POST requests with an invalid SSO assertion to /portal/login
Record the HTTP status of each response
Query the notification stub for alerts sent to the patient
expected: Responses 1 through MAX_ATTEMPTS return HTTP 401 (invalid credential)
Notification stub records exactly one alert for the test patient
trace: R-HLT-098: failed attempts shall be rate-limited
R-HLT-098: failed attempts shall notify the patient
R-HLT-098: error pathway 429 when too many attempts

tests.name: test_last_allowed_attempt_succeeds_and_next_is_rate_limited
preconditions: Rate-limit counter for the test patient is reset to zero
MAX_ATTEMPTS threshold is known (imported from config as MAX_ATTEMPTS)
Invalid SSO assertion available; valid SSO assertion available
actions: Send MAX_ATTEMPTS-1 requests with an invalid SSO assertion (pre-fill counter to boundary-1)
Send the MAX_ATTEMPTS-th request with an invalid SSO assertion (boundary: last 401)
Send the MAX_ATTEMPTS+1-th request with a valid SSO assertion (boundary+1: should be 429)
expected: Request MAX_ATTEMPTS+1 returns HTTP 429 even though a valid assertion is used
No session token is issued on the 429 response
trace: R-HLT-098: rate-limiting boundary — N allowed, N+1 blocked
R-HLT-098: error pathway 429 when too many attempts

tests.name: test_login_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_authenticated_emits_audit
preconditions: Patient is in unauthenticated state (no active session)
actions: Assert no active session exists for the patient (state: unauthenticated)
POST valid SSO assertion to /portal/login (transition: authenticating)
Assert HTTP 200 and session token returned (state: authenticated)
expected: Before login: no session cookie or token present
After login: HTTP 200 with valid session token
AuditEvent outcome '0' records the state transition with correct agent and entity
trace: R-HLT-098: session established on valid SSO
R-HLT-098: AuditEvent emitted end-to-end for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_login_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_authenticated_emits_audit(
    reset_rate_limit, valid_sso_assertion,
    api_client, audit_query, test_patient_id
):
    pre = api_client.get("/portal/session")
    assert pre.status_code in (401, 403), \
        "patient must start in unauthenticated state"

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/portal/login",
        json={"sso_assertion": valid_sso_assertion}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    token = resp.json().get("session_token")
    assert token, "session_token must be present after login"

    post = api_client.get(
        "/portal/session",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    )
    assert post.status_code == 200, "session must be active after login"

    event = audit_query(
        patient_id=test_patient_id, action="login", outcome="0"
    )
    assert event is not None
    assert event["agent"][0]["who"]["reference"] == f"Patient/{test_patient_id}"
